# certinext-zabbix

> CertiNext DCV health monitoring for Zabbix: a trapper-only Zabbix
> template (`templates/template_certinext/7.0/`) plus the
> `certinext-zabbix-push` typer CLI that feeds it. Public, PyPI-ready
> (not yet published), and canonical — `ums-certinext-scripts` (a
> University of Maine System internal repository, not publicly
> accessible) installs this as an independent package rather than
> carrying its own copy.

Start with the [AI-agent quickstart](README.md#ai-agent-quickstart)
section of the README — install command, credential model, and the rule
that `certinext-zabbix-push --dry-run` is the safe way to see what a run
would do.

## Core docs

- [README](README.md): what this is, requirements, install, quickstart
  (import the template, run the pusher), the `KEY_*` ↔ template sync
  rule, development commands.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) / [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): operational facts for
  coding agents — dev setup, test/lint/type-check commands, CI gotchas,
  and the GitLab-canonical/GitHub-mirror relationship.
- [Deployment guide](docs/deployment.md): how to run
  `certinext-zabbix-push` unattended — systemd timer (recommended), cron,
  and Windows Task Scheduler configurations; environment-variable and
  flag reference; the one-time Zabbix template import/link/macro setup;
  network requirements; validation steps; a configuration-management
  (Ansible) task checklist. **Read this before writing any install/deploy
  automation for this repo.**
- [Per-template README](templates/template_certinext/7.0/README.md):
  stands on its own for the "import the template" story — required
  macros, the eight trapper items, the sixteen triggers, and an author
  line, per the Zabbix community-templates conventions.
- [examples/systemd/](examples/systemd/), [examples/cron/](examples/cron/),
  [examples/windows/](examples/windows/): ready-to-adapt unit files,
  cron lines, and Task Scheduler scripts referenced from
  `docs/deployment.md`. `examples/windows/` is explicitly untested —
  written by analogy with the systemd/cron examples, not verified
  against a real Windows host.
- [docs/adr/](docs/adr/): accepted architecture decisions (MADR format)
  — start with ADRs 0001–0006, this repo's foundational decisions
  (standalone repo, copy-now/canonical-later, PyPI-not-published-yet,
  GitHub push mirror, MIT license, community-templates-ready layout).
- [docs/wishlist/](docs/wishlist/): ideas deferred for later, with the
  reasoning for deferring — see `docs/wishlist/README.md` for the index
  and status vocabulary.

## Reference

- [certinext on GitHub](https://github.com/tod-uma/certinext) /
  [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/certinext/): the CertiNext API client
  this pusher drives — its README documents the API surface, credential
  resolution, and known vendor API quirks; its `certinext healthcheck`
  CLI ships in the same install and is the standard API-reachability
  probe.
- [zabbix-utils](https://pypi.org/project/zabbix-utils/): the trapper
  Sender protocol library used to push metric values.
- [Zabbix community-templates guidelines](https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/guidelines/en/thosts/community_templates):
  the submission-ready layout and MIT-only license requirement this
  repo's template subtree conforms to (not yet submitted).
